title: sigesbeckia pringlei subsp. pringlei
intro: Sigesbeckia pringlei subsp. pringlei, also known as Mexican marigold or Mexican strawflower, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. It is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing pain, improving immune function, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
